speechOptions.instruction: Speak in a bright, warm, hopeful tone — like happily introducing something you love to a friend. Gentle, positive energy throughout; never dark or flat.

title: What Can MulmoClaude Do?
description: A three-minute catalog of real MulmoClaude uses: everyday records, work tools, hobby archives, hands-off automation, and an assistant that grows into your own. Launch What video.

beats.text: MulmoClaude is an AI assistant that builds — and raises — your own personal record-keeping apps, through conversation. Everything on screen right now was born because a real user asked for it. Let's tour a few of them in three minutes.

<div class='absolute right-0 top-[88px] z-20 flex flex-col gap-[10px]'>

As many uses
as there are lives.
An AI assistant that builds and raises your personal record apps through conversation. A three-minute tour of what real users do with it.
Weather
Dining guide
Trip planning
Invoicing
Event tracker
Brokerage accounts
Museum archive
Nature journal
To-read pile
MulmoClaude · A catalog of real examples

beats.text: First, everyday records. The dining guide, for example, gets better at picking the next restaurant the more visits you log. One user even asked for a World Cup match tracker. Every card here is an app that exists because one person asked.

beats.text: It's used for work, too. The surprise entry: full double-entry accounting. That one ships built in. Others clear monthly routines like invoicing entirely in conversation. Workplace tools, too, gather as you ask.

beats.text: For hobbies and learning: a museum archive that collects the exhibitions you've seen, and a summer nature journal kept with the kids. And the last card is yours. What would you record? If you can describe it, it fits.

MulmoClaude · A catalog of real examples

beats.text: One user plans the coming week with MulmoClaude every Sunday evening. It reshuffles tasks around the holiday and even drafts Monday morning's email. Overpack the schedule, and it remembers that user's priorities and pushes back. "Nothing flashy — it just reliably stays by my side," they say.

beats.text: And it runs even when you're away. On a rainy morning, say, garden watering has vanished from the daily to-do list. That's the weather forecast and the list being connected. Event reminders and monthly recurring chores keep turning over the same way, untouched.

beats.text: The more you use it, the more it becomes yours. Hand it your old pile of notes, and it's sorted and usable from day one. Teach it your guiding rules, and its suggestions start landing. And before you know it, it has become your own AI assistant.

beats.text: Here are users' words, exactly as they said them. The one that says it all comes from someone who has raised more than twenty collections. "I honestly wonder how I got by without it".

beats.text: With this much on display, it might look like it took programming. But all of it was simply asked for, in conversation. If something doesn't fit, you ask for a fix. You don't bend yourself to an app — you ask to fit your life. That's the whole trick.

HOW TO START
Don't build. Ask.

Not you fitting the app — asking to fit your life.
MulmoClaude · A catalog of real examples

beats.text: MulmoClaude. If you can describe it, it can be recorded — and shaped into an app that's yours alone. Start with one thing you've always meant to organize. For why it's built to be "raised", see "An AI Assistant Nurturing Tool". And for the how-to, the "Collection Creation Demo" awaits.
